title: Non-monotonicity (PostgreSQL incompatible) in decimal literals
description:
### What happens?

(This is part 2/3 of breaking down https://github.com/duckdb/duckdb/issues/14779 into independent and suriviving problems)

```sql
select '7e-15'::DECIMAL(38,10);
```
returns
```
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ CAST('7e-15' AS DECIMAL(38,10)) в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ         decimal(38,10)          в”Ӯ
в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
в”Ӯ                    0.0000000001 в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```
but should return `0`, like PostgreSQL does. Note that DuckDB isn't even monotonic: ` '1e-14'::DECIMAL(38,10);` returns (the correct) `0.0`!


### To Reproduce

DuckDB: https://shell.duckdb.org/#queries=v0,select-'7e%2015'%3A%3ADECIMAL(38%2C10)~
PG: https://www.db-fiddle.com/f/9zoWTQcVwpr1s4NyFYa4yk/0

### OS:

.

'1.2.2-dev15'

bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, value DECIMAL(38,10));
INSERT INTO test_table (id, value) VALUES (1, CAST('7e-15' AS DECIMAL(38,10)));
INSERT INTO test_table (id, value) VALUES (2, CAST('1e-14' AS DECIMAL(38,10)));
SELECT id, value FROM test_table WHERE value IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, value FROM test_table WHERE value < CAST('1e-10' AS DECIMAL(38,10));
key_bug_features: Inconsistent decimal casting for scientific notation literals
